tiltr 0.1.0 documentation

Section Navigation

  • tiltr package

tiltr package#

Submodules#

tiltr.fetch module#

fetch.py#

Utilities to download the TEPCat “all planet info” CSV file from Keele University and cache it locally.

TEPCat homepage: https://www.astro.keele.ac.uk/jkt/tepcat/tepcat.html Direct CSV link : https://www.astro.keele.ac.uk/jkt/tepcat/allinfo-csv.csv

tiltr.fetch.fetch_tepcat_csv(dest_path=None, url='https://www.astro.keele.ac.uk/jkt/tepcat/allinfo-csv.csv', force=False, timeout=30)[source]#

Download the latest TEPCat all-info CSV file and save it locally.

Parameters:

  • dest_path (str | PathLike | None) – Where to save the CSV file. Defaults to ~/.tepcat_lambda_psi/allinfo-csv.csv.

  • url (str) – The URL to fetch the CSV from. Defaults to the official TEPCat “all info” CSV link.

  • force (bool) – If True, always re-download even if a cached copy already exists. If False (default) and a cached file is already present, it is still refreshed (TEPCat is updated frequently), but network errors will fall back to the existing cached copy instead of raising, unless no cached copy exists at all.

  • timeout (int) – Timeout in seconds for the HTTP request.

Returns:

Path to the downloaded (or cached, on failure) CSV file.

Return type:

Path

Raises:

RuntimeError – If the download fails and no cached copy is available to fall back on.

tiltr.plotter module#

plotter.py#

TEPCatPlotter: a class for loading the TEPCat “all planet info” table and plotting the spin-orbit obliquity parameters Lambda (projected obliquity, degrees) and Psi (3D/true obliquity, degrees) against:

  • semi-major axis, a (AU), on a log scale (default)

  • orbital period, P (days), on a log scale (default)

  • stellar effective temperature, Teff (K)

Missing values in TEPCat are encoded as -1 or -999 depending on the column; these are filtered out automatically before plotting. Error bars are added for both axes where available:

  • Lambda / Psi: uses the first error column after each (symmetric).

  • a(AU): uses errup/errdown (asymmetric).

  • Period(day): uses Perioderr (symmetric).

  • Teff: uses the first ‘err’ column (symmetric).

The colour bar shows eccentricity, clamped to the physical range [0, 1].

class tiltr.plotter.TEPCatPlotter(csv_path=None, auto_download=True, url='https://www.astro.keele.ac.uk/jkt/tepcat/allinfo-csv.csv')[source]#

Bases: object

Load TEPCat data and make Lambda / Psi diagnostic plots.

Parameters:

  • csv_path (str | PathLike | None) – Path to a local TEPCat CSV file. If not given, the file is (re)downloaded via tepcat_lambda_psi.fetch.fetch_tepcat_csv().

  • auto_download (bool) – If True (default), download/refresh the CSV automatically when no csv_path is supplied, or when the given path doesn’t exist.

  • url (str) – URL to fetch the CSV from, if downloading.

data#

The full TEPCat table, indexed as read from the CSV.

Type:

pandas.DataFrame

plot_grid(log_scale_a=True, log_scale_period=True, highlight=None, figsize=(16, 9), save_path=None, show=True, kraft=6250.0, ylim=(-10, 190))[source]#

Make a 2x3 grid of plots: rows = Lambda, Psi; columns = a, Period, Teff.

Parameters:

  • log_scale_a (bool) – Use log scale for the semi‑major axis (default True).

  • log_scale_period (bool) – Use log scale for the orbital period (default True).

  • highlight (str | Iterable[str] | None) – System name(s) to highlight with a red star marker.

  • figsize (tuple) – Overall figure size.

  • save_path (str | PathLike | None) – If given, save the figure to this path.

  • show (bool) – If True, call plt.show() at the end.

  • kraft (float | None) – Passed to the Teff subplots; draws a vertical line at this temperature.

  • ylim (tuple | None) – Y-axis limits for all obliquity subplots.

Return type:

Figure

plot_lambda_vs_a(log_scale=True, highlight=None, ax=None, figsize=(10, 6), save_path=None, show=True, ylim=(-10, 190), **kwargs)[source]#

Plot projected obliquity Lambda vs semi-major axis a (AU).

Return type:

Axes

plot_lambda_vs_period(log_scale=True, highlight=None, ax=None, figsize=(10, 6), save_path=None, show=True, ylim=(-10, 190), **kwargs)[source]#

Plot projected obliquity Lambda vs orbital period (days), log scale by default.

Return type:

Axes

plot_lambda_vs_teff(highlight=None, ax=None, figsize=(10, 6), save_path=None, show=True, kraft=6250.0, ylim=(-10, 190), **kwargs)[source]#

Plot projected obliquity Lambda vs stellar Teff (K).

Parameters:

kraft (float | None) – If not None, draw a vertical dashed line at this temperature. The canonical Kraft break is 6250 K.

Return type:

Axes

plot_psi_vs_a(log_scale=True, highlight=None, ax=None, figsize=(10, 6), save_path=None, show=True, ylim=(-10, 190), **kwargs)[source]#

Plot true (3D) obliquity Psi vs semi-major axis a (AU).

Return type:

Axes

plot_psi_vs_period(log_scale=True, highlight=None, ax=None, figsize=(10, 6), save_path=None, show=True, ylim=(-10, 190), **kwargs)[source]#

Plot true (3D) obliquity Psi vs orbital period (days), log scale by default.

Return type:

Axes

plot_psi_vs_teff(highlight=None, ax=None, figsize=(10, 6), save_path=None, show=True, kraft=6250.0, ylim=(-10, 190), **kwargs)[source]#

Plot true (3D) obliquity Psi vs stellar Teff (K).

Parameters:

kraft (float | None) – If not None, draw a vertical dashed line at this temperature. The canonical Kraft break is 6250 K.

Return type:

Axes

refresh(url='https://www.astro.keele.ac.uk/jkt/tepcat/allinfo-csv.csv', force=True)[source]#

Re-download the TEPCat CSV and reload self.data.

Return type:

None

Module contents#

tepcat_lambda_psi#

A small package for fetching the TEPCat “all planet info” table and plotting spin-orbit obliquity diagnostics (Lambda, Psi) against semi-major axis, eccentricity, and stellar effective temperature.

Example

>>> from tepcat_lambda_psi import TEPCatPlotter
>>> tp = TEPCatPlotter()                    # downloads/refreshes the CSV
>>> tp.plot_lambda_vs_teff(highlight="TOI-1842")
>>> tp.plot_grid(highlight="TOI-1842", save_path="obliquity_grid.png")
class tiltr.TEPCatPlotter(csv_path=None, auto_download=True, url='https://www.astro.keele.ac.uk/jkt/tepcat/allinfo-csv.csv')[source]#

Bases: object

Load TEPCat data and make Lambda / Psi diagnostic plots.

Parameters:

  • csv_path (str | PathLike | None) – Path to a local TEPCat CSV file. If not given, the file is (re)downloaded via tepcat_lambda_psi.fetch.fetch_tepcat_csv().

  • auto_download (bool) – If True (default), download/refresh the CSV automatically when no csv_path is supplied, or when the given path doesn’t exist.

  • url (str) – URL to fetch the CSV from, if downloading.

data#

The full TEPCat table, indexed as read from the CSV.

Type:

pandas.DataFrame

plot_grid(log_scale_a=True, log_scale_period=True, highlight=None, figsize=(16, 9), save_path=None, show=True, kraft=6250.0, ylim=(-10, 190))[source]#

Make a 2x3 grid of plots: rows = Lambda, Psi; columns = a, Period, Teff.

Parameters:

  • log_scale_a (bool) – Use log scale for the semi‑major axis (default True).

  • log_scale_period (bool) – Use log scale for the orbital period (default True).

  • highlight (str | Iterable[str] | None) – System name(s) to highlight with a red star marker.

  • figsize (tuple) – Overall figure size.

  • save_path (str | PathLike | None) – If given, save the figure to this path.

  • show (bool) – If True, call plt.show() at the end.

  • kraft (float | None) – Passed to the Teff subplots; draws a vertical line at this temperature.

  • ylim (tuple | None) – Y-axis limits for all obliquity subplots.

Return type:

Figure

plot_lambda_vs_a(log_scale=True, highlight=None, ax=None, figsize=(10, 6), save_path=None, show=True, ylim=(-10, 190), **kwargs)[source]#

Plot projected obliquity Lambda vs semi-major axis a (AU).

Return type:

Axes

plot_lambda_vs_period(log_scale=True, highlight=None, ax=None, figsize=(10, 6), save_path=None, show=True, ylim=(-10, 190), **kwargs)[source]#

Plot projected obliquity Lambda vs orbital period (days), log scale by default.

Return type:

Axes

plot_lambda_vs_teff(highlight=None, ax=None, figsize=(10, 6), save_path=None, show=True, kraft=6250.0, ylim=(-10, 190), **kwargs)[source]#

Plot projected obliquity Lambda vs stellar Teff (K).

Parameters:

kraft (float | None) – If not None, draw a vertical dashed line at this temperature. The canonical Kraft break is 6250 K.

Return type:

Axes

plot_psi_vs_a(log_scale=True, highlight=None, ax=None, figsize=(10, 6), save_path=None, show=True, ylim=(-10, 190), **kwargs)[source]#

Plot true (3D) obliquity Psi vs semi-major axis a (AU).

Return type:

Axes

plot_psi_vs_period(log_scale=True, highlight=None, ax=None, figsize=(10, 6), save_path=None, show=True, ylim=(-10, 190), **kwargs)[source]#

Plot true (3D) obliquity Psi vs orbital period (days), log scale by default.

Return type:

Axes

plot_psi_vs_teff(highlight=None, ax=None, figsize=(10, 6), save_path=None, show=True, kraft=6250.0, ylim=(-10, 190), **kwargs)[source]#

Plot true (3D) obliquity Psi vs stellar Teff (K).

Parameters:

kraft (float | None) – If not None, draw a vertical dashed line at this temperature. The canonical Kraft break is 6250 K.

Return type:

Axes

refresh(url='https://www.astro.keele.ac.uk/jkt/tepcat/allinfo-csv.csv', force=True)[source]#

Re-download the TEPCat CSV and reload self.data.

Return type:

None

tiltr.fetch_tepcat_csv(dest_path=None, url='https://www.astro.keele.ac.uk/jkt/tepcat/allinfo-csv.csv', force=False, timeout=30)[source]#

Download the latest TEPCat all-info CSV file and save it locally.

Parameters:

  • dest_path (str | PathLike | None) – Where to save the CSV file. Defaults to ~/.tepcat_lambda_psi/allinfo-csv.csv.

  • url (str) – The URL to fetch the CSV from. Defaults to the official TEPCat “all info” CSV link.

  • force (bool) – If True, always re-download even if a cached copy already exists. If False (default) and a cached file is already present, it is still refreshed (TEPCat is updated frequently), but network errors will fall back to the existing cached copy instead of raising, unless no cached copy exists at all.

  • timeout (int) – Timeout in seconds for the HTTP request.

Returns:

Path to the downloaded (or cached, on failure) CSV file.

Return type:

Path

Raises:

RuntimeError – If the download fails and no cached copy is available to fall back on.
